name: O'Kims
tagline: Korean fusion with French and Japanese technique — Yelp's #58 US restaurant in 2024. Truffle mandoo that'll rewire your brain.
mustOrder: truffle mandoo — Korean dumplings elevated with truffle
kalbi short ribs — the classic, perfectly executed
black sesame cake — don't skip dessert
parking: Street parking on Nuuanu Ave; paid lots on Smith/Maunakea St
talkStory: Chef Hyun Kim fuses Korean home cooking with French and Japanese technique — trained in fine dining before opening this intimate Chinatown spot around 2019. The truffle mandoo (Korean dumplings with black truffle) became a viral sensation and put the restaurant on Yelp's Top 100 US Restaurants list at #58 in 2024. Family-run with Hyun's wife managing front of house. Extensive vegan menu including house-made vegan kimchi. A genuinely original voice in Honolulu dining.
